goal: Pull eval launch off /project-market cards and onto /projects/[id] as a dev-only ProjectOperator panel. User feedback: card-level Launch eval was scoped wrong â€” accidental scaffolding of new generations when the user expected "show me the latest build." Cards now read-only (View project only). New `ProjectOperator.tsx` client component on /projects/[id] renders: (a) dev-only amber-tinted operator section, (b) status strip with preserved-build info (resolved server-side from apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/v<N>/ via fs walk â€” file count, size, path) + latest-generation info, (c) explicit "Run new eval" button with confirm-first intermediate state that spells out "this will scaffold a NEW eval generation via `gad eval run --project X --execute`. It does NOT run the agent â€” just produces EXEC.json," (d) live 320px terminal panel consuming the existing /api/dev/launch-eval SSE, (e) abort/clear/run-again affordances with exit-code display. Server-side resolvers walk [apps/forge/evals, evals, vendor/get-anything-done/evals] for latest generation and apps/portfolio/public/evals for preserved build. Solves "right now I am blind" complaint â€” user can see what's running, what ran, and what's preserved without leaving the site.
